title: Digest: mol-witness-patrol
description: Patrol cycle 2: all quiet. Obsidian polecat stopped and cleaned up between cycles. No polecats active. No mail, no swarms, no timer gates. Deacon still stopped (known). Refinery running.

title: adj-adjutant-polecat-obsidian
description:
adj-adjutant-polecat-obsidian

role_type: polecat
rig: adjutant
agent_state: closed
hook_bead: null
cleanup_status: null
active_mr: null
notification_level: null

title: Digest: mol-witness-patrol
description: Patrol cycle 1: all quiet. Refinery running, no MRs queued. No polecats initially; obsidian appeared mid-patrol (status=done, idle at prompt). Nudged to start. Deacon stopped (known). No swarms, no timer gates, no cleanup wisps.

title: AdjutantV2: Session Bridge
description: Remote Claude Code multiplexer. Tunnel to real terminal sessions via tmux pipe-pane + WebSocket.

title: Phase 1: Session Bridge Core
description: Backend only. Get tmux <-> WebSocket working. Deliverable: Connect to an existing tmux session from a WebSocket client and see live output, send input.

title: SessionRegistry: in-memory + file persistence
description: Track all known sessions. ManagedSession interface with id, name, tmuxSession, tmuxPane, projectPath, mode, status, connectedClients, outputBuffer, pipeActive. Persist to ~/.adjutant/sessions.json.

title: SessionConnector: tmux pipe-pane output streaming
description: Attach to tmux sessions via pipe-pane, stream output through FIFO. Buffer partial lines, pass complete lines downstream. Stop pipe-pane when no clients connected. Periodic capture-pane for reconnect preview.

title: InputRouter: tmux send-keys input + interrupt
description: Route WebSocket input to tmux send-keys. Handle session_input and session_interrupt (Ctrl-C). Input queuing while agent is mid-response. FIFO delivery when agent becomes idle.

title: LifecycleManager: create/kill tmux sessions
description: Create tmux sessions with Claude Code. Kill sessions on demand. Workspace setup (primary, worktree, copy). Handle session discovery on startup.

title: WebSocket protocol v2: session_* message types
description: Extend WS protocol with session_list, session_create, session_connect, session_disconnect, session_input, session_interrupt, session_kill, session_permission. Server responses: session_list, session_created, session_output, session_raw, session_status, session_permission, session_ended.

title: Basic output relay: raw terminal bytes
description: Stream raw terminal bytes from tmux to WebSocket clients. Ring buffer (1000 lines) per session. Replay/skip on reconnect. No parsing yet.

title: Phase 10: GT Integration
description:
Connect the Session Bridge to Gas Town. Auto-discover GT tmux sessions (mayor, polecats, witnesses), register them in SessionRegistry, and allow iOS to observe/inject into GT agent sessions.

## Deliverable
Connect to GT, see mayor's live output stream, switch between GT agents, create polecats from iOS.

## Current State
- tmux.ts has listTmuxSessions(), gt-executor.ts has gt.agents.list
- SessionBridge init() only handles standalone mode — no GT discovery
- CrewListView shows REST-fetched agent data, not live session streaming

title: GT session auto-discovery and registration
description:
On startup (and periodically), the session bridge should discover GT sessions by:
1. Reading town.json for rig list
2. Scanning for tmux sessions matching GT naming (mayor, deacon, witness, rig polecats)
3. Cross-referencing with gt agents list for status
4. Auto-registering discovered sessions in SessionRegistry

## Work Required
- Add a discoverGTSessions() method to SessionBridge (or LifecycleManager)
- Call it in init() when mode is 'gastown'
- Set up periodic re-scan (e.g., every 30s) to catch new polecats
- Map GT agent names to session names (e.g., 'mayor' → tmux session 'mayor')

## Existing Files
- backend/src/services/session-bridge.ts
- backend/src/services/tmux.ts - listTmuxSessions()
- backend/src/services/gt-executor.ts - gt.agents.list
- backend/src/services/gastown-workspace.ts - loadTownConfig(), loadRigsConfig()
- backend/src/services/session-registry.ts

title: Observe mode: read-only session connections
description:
Add an observe mode for session connections where the client can see live output but cannot send input. This is critical for GT integration where you want to watch agents without interfering.

## Work Required
- Add 'mode' field to session_connect WS message: { sessionId, replay?, mode: 'observe' | 'inject' }
- In observe mode, InputRouter rejects session_input messages for this client
- Multiple observers can watch the same session simultaneously
- iOS should show a visual indicator (e.g., 'OBSERVING' vs 'CONNECTED')

## Existing Files
- backend/src/services/ws-server.ts - handles session_connect
- backend/src/services/input-router.ts - routes input to tmux
- backend/src/services/session-registry.ts - tracks connectedClients

title: Crew view with live session status
description:
CrewListView currently shows REST-fetched agent data. It should show live session status when in GT mode, allowing tap-to-connect to any agent's live terminal.

## Work Required
- When in gastown mode, CrewListView should show session status (idle/working/waiting_permission) from SessionRegistry
- Tap an agent row → connect to their session (observe or inject mode)
- Show polecat creation button that triggers gt sling from iOS
- POST /agents/spawn-polecat endpoint and iOS API endpoint already exist but no UI flow triggers them

## Existing Files
- ios/Adjutant/Features/Crew/CrewListView.swift
- ios/Adjutant/Features/Crew/CrewDetailView.swift
- backend/src/routes/agents.ts - POST /agents/spawn-polecat
- ios/AdjutantKit/Sources/AdjutantKit/Networking/APIClient+Endpoints.swift

title: Phase 11: Swarm Communication
description:
Add agent-to-agent communication within swarms. The spec calls for 'shared beads or file-based' communication between swarm agents. Currently swarm agents are isolated — they share a git repo via worktrees but have no way to coordinate.

## Deliverable
Swarm agents can discover each other and exchange messages/status, enabling coordinator pattern to actually orchestrate work.

title: Agent-to-agent communication in swarms
description:
Implement a communication mechanism for swarm agents. Options:
1. Shared file-based mailbox (each agent has an inbox file in the project root)
2. Shared beads database (agents read/write beads issues to coordinate)
3. Backend-mediated messaging (agents POST to API, backend routes to other agents)

## Work Required
- Design and implement communication channel
- Coordinator agent should be able to assign tasks to worker agents
- Worker agents should be able to report completion/status to coordinator
- Keep it simple — file-based is likely easiest for v2

## Existing Files
- backend/src/services/swarm-service.ts - tracks swarm agents
- backend/src/services/input-router.ts - can send input to any session

title: Phase 12: Web Frontend Parity
description:
The web frontend (React/Vite) has no v2 features. It needs session bridge support, swarm management, permission settings, and cost tracking views to match iOS capabilities.

## Deliverable
Web frontend has feature parity with iOS for all v2 features.

## Current State
Web frontend is fully functional for v1 features (mail, beads, crew, dashboard, settings, voice, terminal) with CRT-themed UI. Built with React 19, TypeScript, Vite, Tailwind, xterm.js. Has SSE/WebSocket support for real-time updates but no session bridge integration.

title: Web: Session bridge integration (connect/stream/input)
description:
Add session bridge support to the web frontend. The web already has WebSocket and xterm.js for terminal display.

## Work Required
- Add session_connect/disconnect/input/interrupt WS message types to web WS client
- Add session list view showing all active sessions
- Add session chat view with dual mode (structured chat + xterm.js terminal)
- Connect existing TerminalPane component to session_raw output
- Add session_permission handling with approval dialog

## Existing Files
- frontend/src/ - React app with WebSocket support
- frontend/src/components/Terminal/TerminalPane.tsx - xterm.js terminal
- frontend/src/components/Chat/ - existing chat components

title: Web: Swarm, permissions, and cost views
description:
Add remaining v2 features to web frontend to match iOS.

## Work Required
- Swarm management view (create, scale, branch status, merge)
- Permission settings view (per-agent/per-tool config)
- Cost tracking dashboard (per-session, per-project costs, alert threshold)
- Projects management view (list, create, activate, delete)

## Existing Backend APIs
- /api/swarms/* - full CRUD
- /api/permissions/* - config CRUD
- /api/costs/* - cost data and thresholds
- /api/projects/* - project CRUD

title: Phase 2: Output Parser
description: Make the raw stream usable for a chat UI. Deliverable: WebSocket clients receive both session_output (structured) and session_raw (terminal bytes) for the same session.

title: ANSI escape code stripper
description: Strip ANSI escape codes for chat mode rendering. Use strip-ansi or equivalent. Keep raw bytes for terminal mode.

title: Claude Code output pattern recognizer
description: Recognize Claude Code output patterns: tool use markers (Read/Edit/Bash), agent messages, tool results, permission prompts, status indicators (thinking/working), cost/token output. Best-effort parsing.

title: Dual emission: structured events + raw bytes
description: Emit both OutputEvent[] (structured) and raw string for each chunk. Client chooses which to render. OutputEvent types: message, tool_use, tool_result, status, permission_request, error, raw.

title: Phase 3: iOS Chat Overhaul
description: Replace mail-based chat with session-connected chat. Deliverable: Open Adjutant, tap an agent, see its live output, type prompts.

title: SessionChatView: WebSocket v2 session-connected chat
description: New SwiftUI view that connects to a session via WebSocket v2. Replaces mail-based ChatView for live sessions. Shows streaming output and accepts input.

title: Hybrid renderer: chat bubbles + tool cards
description: Render structured OutputEvents as chat bubbles (agent messages) and collapsible tool cards (Read/Edit/Bash). Show tool inputs and truncated results.

title: SwiftTerm integration: terminal mode toggle
description: Embed SwiftTerm widget for raw terminal view. Toggle between chat mode and terminal mode. Send raw bytes to SwiftTerm. Full ANSI color support.

title: WebSocket v2 client: session message support
description: Extend iOS WebSocketClient to handle session_* message types. Session connect/disconnect, input sending, interrupt, permission responses.

title: Stop button (Ctrl-C) + reconnect replay option
description: Stop button visible while agent is working (sends session_interrupt). On reconnect, offer replay buffer vs skip to present. Connection indicator: LIVE/BUFFERED/OFFLINE.

title: Phase 4: Session Lifecycle
description: Create, manage, and kill sessions from iOS. Deliverable: Create a project, start an agent, chat with it, stop it. All from iOS.

title: Projects REST API: CRUD + clone + activate
description: POST /api/projects (create from path, clone URL, or empty). GET /api/projects (list). POST /api/projects/:id/activate. DELETE /api/projects/:id. Persist to ~/.adjutant/projects.json.

title: Sessions REST API: create/list/kill
description: POST /api/sessions (create tmux + claude). GET /api/sessions (list active). DELETE /api/sessions/:id (kill). Workspace setup: worktree, copy, or primary.

title: iOS: Project management view
description: New top-level view listing projects with agent counts. Create project (clone/open/new). Tap project to see agents, start/stop, switch modes.

title: iOS: Agent switcher + start/stop controls
description: Sidebar or bottom sheet listing all active sessions with status. Tap to switch. Start/stop agent buttons. Show session status (IDLE/WORKING/PERMISSION).

title: Phase 5: Swarm Mode
description: Multi-agent orchestration without GT infrastructure. Deliverable: Spin up N agents on one project, watch them work in parallel, merge results.

title: Swarm creation: N agents in worktrees
description: Create N tmux sessions + worktrees for one project. Each agent gets its own branch. Configurable workspace strategy (worktree vs copy).

title: Coordinator pattern: designated merge agent
description: One agent designated as coordinator. Reviews and merges other agents work. Lightweight refinery pattern.

title: Dynamic scaling: add/remove agents from iOS
description: Add Agent creates new worktree + tmux session. Remove Agent kills session, optionally removes worktree. Live agent count in UI.

title: Branch merge UI: see branches, trigger merges
description: Show agent branches in iOS. Trigger merge back to main. Conflict detection and resolution flow.

title: Phase 6: Permissions & Polish
description: Permission system, cost tracking, session persistence, bandwidth optimization.

title: Permission system: config + detection + routing
description: Per-agent permission config (auto-accept / manual). Detect permission prompts in output parser. Route permission_request to iOS. Send y/n response via tmux send-keys.

title: iOS: Permission dialog + push notifications
description: Show permission dialog when agent requests approval. Push notification if app is backgrounded. Permission response routing back to backend.

title: Cost/token tracking: parse + store + stream
description: Parse cost/token output from Claude Code sessions. Store in ~/.adjutant/costs.json. Stream via WebSocket session_cost events. Per-session and per-project aggregation. Cost alerts.

title: Session persistence + bandwidth optimization
description: Survive backend restarts (reconnect to tmux sessions). Throttle/buffer raw output for slow clients. Delta compression. Persistent pipe-pane log files for full history.

title: Phase 7: Projects View & Navigation
description:
Wire the Projects view into iOS navigation and ensure project CRUD works end-to-end. Projects is the gateway to sessions, swarms, and agent management — it needs to be a first-class tab or accessible from the main UI.

## Deliverable
Open Adjutant, see Projects tab, create/clone/open projects, start agents from project detail view.

## Key Gaps
- ProjectsListView and ProjectDetailView exist but are NOT in the tab bar or any navigation path
- Project clone-from-URL and empty project creation need verification
- Project detail should link to sessions and swarm management for that project

title: Add Projects tab to iOS MainTabView
description:
Add Projects as a tab in MainTabView.swift (or as a navigation destination from Dashboard/Settings). ProjectsListView and ProjectDetailView already exist but are unreachable.

## Work Required
- Add Projects tab to MainTabView (visible in standalone and swarm modes)
- Wire AppCoordinator to handle project navigation paths
- ProjectsListView should be the tab root, ProjectDetailView on tap

## Existing Files
- ios/Adjutant/Features/Projects/ProjectsListView.swift
- ios/Adjutant/Features/Projects/ProjectDetailView.swift
- ios/Adjutant/Features/Projects/ProjectRowView.swift
- ios/Adjutant/Core/Navigation/MainTabView.swift

title: Verify and fix project clone/empty creation
description:
Verify that the projects-service.ts createProject() handles all three creation modes from AdjutantV2.md:
1. Create from existing directory (POST /api/projects { path })
2. Create by cloning (POST /api/projects { cloneUrl, name })
3. Create empty (POST /api/projects { name, empty: true })

Fix any missing paths. Also verify ProjectDetailView has a 'Start Agent' button that creates a session for the project.

## Existing Files
- backend/src/services/projects-service.ts
- backend/src/routes/projects.ts
- ios/Adjutant/Features/Projects/ProjectDetailView.swift

title: Link project detail to sessions and swarm management
description:
ProjectDetailView should be the hub for a project's agents. From project detail, users should be able to:
- See active sessions for this project
- Start a new standalone agent (creates session)
- Start a swarm (creates swarm with N agents)
- Navigate to SwarmView for swarm management
- Navigate to SessionChatView to interact with an agent

## Existing Files
- ios/Adjutant/Features/Projects/ProjectDetailView.swift
- ios/Adjutant/Features/Projects/ProjectDetailViewModel.swift
- ios/Adjutant/Features/Sessions/SessionChatView.swift
- ios/Adjutant/Features/Swarm/SwarmView.swift

title: Phase 8: Service Wiring & Pipeline Integration
description:
Connect the existing services into a working pipeline. All the pieces exist (OutputParser, CostTracker, PermissionService, OutputThrottle) but they aren't wired into the session output flow.

## Deliverable
Session output flows through: SessionConnector → OutputParser → OutputThrottle → WebSocket, with CostTracker and PermissionService tapped into the pipeline. Clients receive both structured and raw output.

## Current State
Each service works in isolation with unit tests passing. The session bridge orchestrates registry/connector/input/lifecycle but doesn't route output through parser, throttle, permissions, or cost tracking.

title: Wire OutputParser into SessionConnector output handler
description:
SessionConnector captures raw terminal output from tmux pipe-pane. This raw output needs to flow through OutputParser to produce structured events.

## Work Required
- In session-bridge.ts (or session-connector output handler), pipe each line through OutputParser
- Emit both session_output (structured OutputEvent[]) and session_raw (raw bytes) to connected WS clients
- This is the 'dual emission' requirement from AdjutantV2.md spec

## Existing Files
- backend/src/services/session-bridge.ts - orchestrator, has output handler
- backend/src/services/session-connector.ts - captures raw output
- backend/src/services/output-parser.ts - converts raw → structured events
- backend/src/services/ws-server.ts - broadcasts to WS clients

title: Wire CostTracker to receive cost_update events from OutputParser
description:
OutputParser emits cost_update events when it sees token/cost lines in session output. CostTracker has recordCostUpdate() but nothing calls it.

## Work Required
- When OutputParser emits a cost_update event, call recordCostUpdate() with the session ID, project path, and cost data
- This can be done in the session bridge output handler or via EventBus subscription
- CostTracker already emits session:cost and session:cost_alert events via EventBus

## Existing Files
- backend/src/services/output-parser.ts - emits cost_update events
- backend/src/services/cost-tracker.ts - has recordCostUpdate()
- backend/src/services/session-bridge.ts - orchestrator
- backend/src/services/event-bus.ts - pub/sub

title: Wire PermissionService into session output pipeline
description:
PermissionService has processOutputLine() which feeds per-session OutputParsers and auto-handles or routes permission prompts. But nothing calls processOutputLine() with actual session output.

## Work Required
- Route session output lines through PermissionService.processOutputLine()
- When a permission_request is detected and mode is 'manual', it should emit via EventBus for the WS layer to send to iOS
- When mode is 'auto_accept' or 'auto_deny', it should auto-respond via InputRouter (send 'y' or 'n')
- Coordinate with adj-v2.8.1 (OutputParser wiring) — permission service may consume parser events instead of raw lines

## Existing Files
- backend/src/services/permission-service.ts - has processOutputLine(), getEffectiveMode()
- backend/src/services/input-router.ts - sends y/n to tmux
- backend/src/services/session-bridge.ts - orchestrator

title: Integrate OutputThrottle into WebSocket relay
description:
OutputThrottle buffers and batches output lines per session, flushing at configurable intervals (default 100ms) or when batch is full (default 50 lines). It exists but isn't inserted into the WS relay path.

## Work Required
- Insert OutputThrottle between the output pipeline and WebSocket broadcast
- Raw output from SessionConnector → OutputParser → OutputThrottle → WS broadcast
- OutputThrottle.onFlush() callback should send batched events to connected WS clients
- This prevents overwhelming slow mobile clients with rapid terminal output

## Existing Files
- backend/src/services/output-throttle.ts - has push(), onFlush(), shutdown()
- backend/src/services/ws-server.ts - broadcasts to clients
- backend/src/services/session-bridge.ts - orchestrator

title: Phase 9: iOS Navigation Wiring
description:
Wire orphaned iOS views into the app navigation. SwarmView and PermissionSettingsView exist with full implementations but are unreachable from the tab bar or any navigation path. (SessionChatView is being handled separately.)

## Deliverable
All v2 iOS views are accessible from the app UI — swarm management from project detail, permission settings from Settings tab.

title: Wire SwarmView into iOS navigation
description:
SwarmView exists with full swarm management UI (create, scale, branch merge) but is not accessible from any navigation path.

## Work Required
- SwarmView should be reachable from ProjectDetailView when a project has an active swarm
- Add 'Start Team' button in ProjectDetailView that creates a swarm and navigates to SwarmView
- Optionally add swarm overview to Dashboard if swarms are active
- Register navigation path in AppCoordinator

## Existing Files
- ios/Adjutant/Features/Swarm/SwarmView.swift
- ios/Adjutant/Features/Swarm/SwarmViewModel.swift
- ios/Adjutant/Features/Projects/ProjectDetailView.swift
- ios/Adjutant/Core/Navigation/MainTabView.swift

title: Wire PermissionSettingsView into Settings tab
description:
PermissionSettingsView has full per-agent permission configuration UI but is unreachable.

## Work Required
- Add a 'Permissions' row in SettingsView.swift that navigates to PermissionSettingsView
- Register navigation path in AppCoordinator
- Ensure the permission dialog modal can be presented from SessionChatView when a permission_request WS event arrives

## Existing Files
- ios/Adjutant/Features/Permissions/PermissionSettingsView.swift
- ios/Adjutant/Features/Permissions/PermissionSettingsViewModel.swift
- ios/Adjutant/Features/Settings/SettingsView.swift
